title: USD Composition Opinion Stack Capability
purpose: Define the target-native opinion-stack and prim-index boundary that central composition providers expose after collecting local layer-stack opinions and composition-arc opinions. This capability is the contract that prevents stage population, nanousd backends, or individual arc providers from owning final LIVERPS arbitration.

included: strong-to-weak prim and property opinion stacks
target-native prim and property indexes for composed scene paths
composition arc kind, layer-stack identity, source spec path, exposed scene path, and namespace mapping provenance
LIVERPS family ordering across local, inherits, variants, relocates, references, payloads, and specializes opinions
same-layer-stack strength ordering by layer strength
same-arc-family ordering by namespace depth, authored versus implied arcs, and authored list order when represented by the source
namespace-remapped path-valued field contents in composition opinions before those opinions feed stage population or generated C API backends
bounded recursive reference opinion closure when supplied by bounded reference sources
recursive and implied inherit or specialize opinion closure when supplied by bounded inherits and specializes sources
selected variant opinions, including recursively selected variant opinions when supplied by the variant source, inserted at variant strength after selection is computed
specializes opinions represented as globally weakest opinions
namespace-source facade produced from composed opinion stacks for stage population
deferred: requiring a particular C++ class, ABI layout, allocator, cache, or OpenUSD PCP data structure
recursive arc discovery beyond the arc opinions supplied by the graph
payload asset resolution, layer opening, and load policy
external variant fallback maps or stage/session policies not defined by the AOUSD v1.0.1 core composition text
implied inherits and specializes that require layer-stack context not represented by consumed bounded sources
dictionary, listOp, time-sample, spline, clip, and value-resolution semantics beyond exposing strongest authored field opinions
schema fallback properties, complete instancing/prototypes, model hierarchy policy, and value clips

model.CompositionArbitrator: The target-native provider that consumes local and composition-arc opinion sources, builds prim/property opinion indexes, and exposes a final NamespaceSource facade.
model.ComposedPrimIndex: A target-native index for one composed prim path. It contains a strong-to-weak list of contributing prim opinions plus composed child names, property names, diagnostics, and source capability declarations.
model.ComposedPropertyIndex: A target-native index for one composed property path. It contains a strong-to-weak list of contributing attribute or relationship opinions.
model.CompositionOpinion: A target-native record for one contributing prim or property spec after namespace mapping. It stores arc family, source identifier, layer-stack identity when available, source spec path, exposed scene path, field map, child/property names when applicable, and strength keys.
model.CompositionArcFamily: A token or enum identity for local, inherits, variants, relocates, references, payloads, specializes, or an extension arc family. This is not a raw string in domain storage.
model.StrengthKey: A comparable target-native key derived from AOUSD v1.0.1 strength rules. It may be explicit comparator logic rather than a materialized integer, but it must preserve the specified ordering.
model.NamespaceMapping: A typed mapping from source namespace paths to composed target namespace paths. Source and target entries are semantic path values.

strength_order.same_layer_stack_rule: Opinions in the same layer stack are ordered by layer-stack strength, with the stronger layer contributing the stronger opinion.
same_arc_family_rules: An opinion introduced by an arc authored deeper in namespace is stronger than one introduced higher in namespace.
An authored arc is stronger than an implied arc when both are represented by the source.
Otherwise, sibling arc ordering follows the source-visible composed list order for that arc family.
strength_order.variant_rule: Variant selections are computed only after other composition arcs have supplied the opinions needed to choose the selection; selected variant opinions are then inserted at variant strength.
strength_order.specializes_rule: Specializes arc opinions are globally weaker than the opinions on the specializing prim and weaker than opinions introduced by composition arcs on the specialized prim.

output.diagnostics: composition diagnostics relevant to this path
composition diagnostics relevant to this path

laws: the composition arbitrator owns final LIVERPS arbitration; individual arc providers must not be treated as the final authority for field strength against other arc families
stage population must consume the namespace-source facade produced from composed opinion stacks and must not evaluate composition arcs itself
a generated backend such as nanousd must not bypass the composition arbitrator to form its own final prim/property opinions when more than one composition source is in scope

generated domain logic must preserve enough typed opinion provenance to compare arc family, layer strength, source spec path, exposed scene path, namespace mapping, and source-visible list order before producing population-facing fields
generated domain logic must not collapse local, inherits, variants, relocates, references, payloads, and specializes outputs into one field map before applying LIVERPS strength ordering

composition arc family identities, field keys, child names, property names, variant set names, selected variant names, and type names used for composition decisions must be tokens or semantic name handles, not raw strings
source spec paths, exposed scene paths, namespace mapping paths, arc authoring paths, target paths, and property paths must be semantic path values or equivalent target-native path references

path-valued field contents in composed opinions, including listOp<ObjectPath> targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be expressed in composed scene coordinates before the namespace-source facade is exposed; out-of-subtree elements may be preserved only for same-namespace arcs and must be pruned for external or variant-selection source namespaces with no composed identity mapping
namespace remapping of path-valued field contents is required even in the first bounded composition arbitrator; it is not full listOp algebra, dictionary merging, or attribute value resolution
adapter JSON, diagnostics, reports, and C ABI strings may render composition opinions as text, but those strings are boundary representations and must not be parsed back for composition decisions

payload opinions may participate in the stack only when supplied by a loaded payload opinion source; absence of a payload source must produce a partial-composition capability declaration rather than silently claiming loaded payload composition
variants must be selected from source-visible strength-ordered opinions before selected variant opinions are inserted at variant strength

when bounded reference, inherit, or specialize sources supply recursive or implied opinions, the composition arbitrator must compare those opinions by their provenance, namespace depth, source-visible order, and LIVERPS family strength instead of treating them as a flattened field map
specializes opinions must remain globally weakest even when a bounded graph evaluates specializes before variants so variant selection can observe non-variant opinions
composed child and property ordering must be derived from the ordered opinion stack; stage population may only preserve composed ordering and apply population-mask or active pruning

for the first bounded composition arbitrator, non-special field maps may expose the strongest authored field value per field token; dictionary merging, listOp value composition, and value resolution require later contracts before a provider may claim them
